context.summary: Use micro:bit芒聙聶s radio feature to answer questions in secret.
how_it_works: Flash this program on to two micro:bits. You and a friend ask each other questions that have 芒聙聵yes芒聙聶 or 芒聙聵no芒聙聶 as the answer.
Press input button A to send 芒聙聵yes芒聙聶 and button B to send 芒聙聵no芒聙聶 messages. A tick or cross will flash on both micro:bits芒聙聶 LED display outputs for half a second.
The program uses radio to send your answer secretly - no-one (except your partner) can hear the radio signal.

When a radio message is received, the program uses selection to test the message: if the received message is equal to 芒聙聵yes芒聙聶, then it shows a tick on the LED display, but if the message is equal to 芒聙聵no芒聙聶 then it shows a cross.
Make sure the radio group number is the same on both micro:bits 芒聙聯 you can use any number between 0 and 255.
If lots of you are using this program in the same place, you芒聙聶ll want to make sure each pair of people has their own radio group number.
Keep your radio group number a secret if you don't want anyone snooping on your messages!
improve_it: Show different icons or messages for 芒聙聵yes芒聙聶 and 芒聙聵no芒聙聶.
Use shake, tilt or buttons A and B together to send different answers such as 芒聙聵maybe.芒聙聶
Change 芒聙聵yes芒聙聶 and 芒聙聵no芒聙聶 to 芒聙聵dot芒聙聶 and 芒聙聵dash芒聙聶 and send Morse code messages.

micropython.source:
from microbit import *
import radio
radio.config(group=7)
radio.on()

while True:
    message = radio.receive()
    if message:
        if message == 'yes':
            display.show(Image.YES)
            sleep(500)
            display.clear()
        elif message == 'no':
            display.show(Image.NO)
            sleep(500)
            display.clear()
    if button_a.was_pressed():
        radio.send('yes')
        display.show(Image.YES)
        sleep(500)
        display.clear()
    if button_b.was_pressed():
        radio.send('no')
        display.show(Image.NO)
        sleep(500)
        display.clear()
